subject: Andy's new veterans jobs plan
body:
Tomorrow is the Fourth of July, a day for all of us to come together with family, enjoy Kentucky's summer, and reflect on the history of our great nation and the men and women who built it.
|

| | Peter,

Tomorrow is the Fourth of July, a day for all of us to come together with family, enjoy Kentucky's summer, and reflect on the history of our great nation and the men and women who built it.

But before the holiday weekend, Andy Beshear got to work. This week, he **proudly announced a new veterans jobs plan** to make sure Kentucky does right by our veterans and creates more good-paying jobs for those who have served our country.

[**Can you take our survey and let us know your thoughts on Andy 's plan to help veterans find good-paying jobs?**](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557b5c/ad6dc73/15706856/db40a5b/2185034234/VEsH/ "https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/9X5PGFK") Andy wants to hear from Kentuckians like you as he advocates for policies that will help our veterans receive the support they deserve.

So before you step away from your inbox and celebrate the Fourth of July, take a moment to [**tell us your thoughts about how a Beshear/Coleman administration can fight for our veterans**](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557b5c/ad6dc73/15706856/db40a5b/2185034234/VEsE/ "https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/9X5PGFK"). We look forward to hearing from you!

Thank you,

name: Dan Baer
subject: Life, Liberty and the pursuit of Happiness.
body:
Hey folks,

I hope June is going well for you!

The month is off to a great start for Team Dan. A big thank you to everyone who has pitched in to help the campaign in the last few weeks. It means a lot. So thank you.

We just got our official campaign store up and running. If you're looking for some Pride gear to wear to a parade, festival, or summer barbeque this month, you should definitely check out our Dan For Colorado Pride shirts. They're pretty great.

>>store.danforcolorado.com/

On a related note, earlier this month we asked people to share with us what Pride Month means to them, and the answers we've gotten back--from folks in Colorado and across the country-- have been really thoughtful and incredibly inspiring.

We're going to start sharing some of these over Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter (follow us for updates!), but we wanted to give you a sneak preview of what some folks said before sharing with the general public:

What does Pride mean to you?

-- "Loving my granddaughter. Unconditionally." - Barbara (CA)

-- "Pride is being true to yourself and living unabashedly happy regardless of what others may think or say." - Ryan (CO)

-- "It means that a number of dear friends who are LGBTQ can at last enjoy the exact same rights, privileges, protections and freedoms as anyone else. This is huge and in my opinion it's ABOUT DAGGONE TIME!" - Robbi (MI)

Those are just a few, but if you want to share yours with us and be featured online, we'd love to hear what Pride means to you.

>>go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/pride-survey-all?source=20190613_em_fr_b2_baer_all

With all this in mind, it seems like a good time to address some of the questions that I've been asked my entire career:

-- "How do you see the significance of being one of the first openly gay people to be nominated as a U.S. ambassador?"

-- "What's it like to be a gay cabinet member?"

-- "Should it matter that you'd be the first openly-gay man in the U.S. Senate?"

Should it matter? No.

Does it matter? Yeah I guess so. But maybe not in the way folks first think it does.

It matters because while I carried around a lot of pain and fear for many years, the empathy forged in the heart of a boy who worried about living an open and happy life has been a gift. I have endeavored to bring that empathy to my life of public service every single day.

And it matters because I believe in the power of progress.

I am the beneficiary of the efforts of so many activists and leaders and advocates who came before me. They made my life possible. Anyone who has been as fortunate as I have been, and who has lived the life I've been blessed to lead can't be cynical about the possibility for progress.

But progress is only possible if and when we fight for it.

When we look at our country today, we see powerful people attacking those who have been marginalized their entire lives. People of color. Immigrants. Women. LGBTQ people.

Every day, too many of our fellow Coloradans, too many of our fellow Americans are fighting. They fight for a fair shake in an unfair world. They fight for the equality that has long been denied them.

But these fights are not theirs alone any more than the fight for LGBTQ rights is a fight for LGBTQ people alone. These fights belong to all of us.

That's why I'm running for the US Senate. Because the values held dear by communities across our state and our country are under threat. And because we all need to have each other's backs, to take care of one another as we rise to meet this moment we're living through.

So why does it matter that I would be the first openly-gay man in the U.S. Senate?

It matters because I will never stop fighting for those who aren't getting a fair shake or a fair shot for those who are left out or left behind.

I won't stop fighting for the teacher who can't afford a one bedroom apartment and works a second job but still pours her heart, soul, and wallet into her classroom.

I won't stop fighting for the student who works full-time while getting a degree, all the while staring down the prospect of a lifetime's worth of crippling student loans.

And I won't stop fighting for the woman who watches politicians in Washington try to legislate her body on a daily basis.

That's why I'm here. That's what I'm fighting for. I believe in the promise of life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness for every person-- but I know that in each generation that promise must be advanced and renewed. Thank you for joining me in this fight.

>>secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190613_em_fr_b2_baer_all&amount=19&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on

Thanks again for your support.

-- Dan

-------

https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com

name: Tom Cotton
subject: Happy Fourth of July
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY COTTON FOR SENATE
body:
Cotton For Senate

As you celebrate Independence Day, take a moment to remember
those who sacrificed everything to defend our freedoms.

Friend,

I just wanted to take a moment to wish you and your family a
Happy Fourth of July!

As I reflect on Independence Day, I think back to the early days
of my career when I was in law school. At the time, I had no
plans to run for office, and was on track to become an attorney.

But watching the twin towers fall on 9/11 forever changed my
trajectory. My reaction was to join the Army, where I served in
the 101st Airborne in Iraq, at Arlington National Cemetery, and
then voluntarily deployed to Afghanistan with a Provincial
Reconstruction Team.

Before I was fighting for our freedoms in the Senate, I was
fighting to defend them overseas, and I know what it costs. I was
fortunate enough to make it home, but there are many who did not.

So please, as you celebrate Independence Day, take a
moment to remember those who sacrificed everything to defend our
freedoms.

In Service,

Tom Cotton
United States Senator

Senator Cotton was honorably discharged from the U.S. Army.
Images do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or
any service branch.

Little Rock, AR 72217-7504

subject: Happy July 4th!

body:
Peter,

**Happy Independence Day!**

The dream of our founders is a nation of opportunity for all. Each day we work hard to make that dream a possibility for those in Central Virginia and the rest of our country.

Today, I'm excited to spend time with my family and join a parade -- one of my favorite things to do on the Fourth of July!

As we celebrate today, I hope you have a great Independence Day!

_\- Abigail_

Today we celebrate the accomplishments of our Founding Fathers, and the ideals of this great country. Our nation has come so far since 1776, and I am excited to continue that work, fighting for a government that will work for us all.

As we celebrate our independence, I hope you will join me in reflecting on the incredible journey our country has taken these last 243 years, and enjoy spending time with friends and family. If you’re like me, your day will be filled with backyard BBQs, block parties, and fireworks shows.

And, as with every good celebration, I’ll be listening to some great music. Celebrate in true #TeamTomlinson style and enjoy a list of tunes curated by our team! [https://open.spotify.com/playlist/2VjwN4rZcgf0mi2Bsa5bLX?si=8tGw-FjQRVi95AWHo348iA]

name: Jim Hood
subject: Happy Fourth of July!
body:
|
---
Today, I'm reaching out to wish you and your loved ones a happy Fourth of July. Every year, this holiday is an opportunity to reflect on how we are blessed to live in the United States, a country in which independence, freedom, and opportunity are the bedrock of our government.
Friend,

Today, I'm reaching out to wish you and your loved ones a happy Fourth of July. Every year, this holiday is an opportunity to reflect on how we are blessed to live in the United States, a country in which independence, freedom, and opportunity are the bedrock of our government.

There's no question in my mind that Mississippi is the greatest state in our union. That's why I'm running for governor -- because I want to grow our economy, improve our public education system, and make health care more affordable and accessible to take Mississippi to its full potential.

I'll be sharing my vision for a better Mississippi today as I celebrate the holiday by attending the 40th Annual Jacinto Fourth of July Festival. And after a long day with supporters and volunteers on the campaign trail, Debbie and I are looking forward to gathering with friends and family for a backyard barbecue.

Whether you're watching fireworks, taking in a parade, or relaxing in your own backyard, I hope that you and your loved ones are celebrating all that makes our nation, and Mississippi, great.

From me, Debbie, and all of Team Hood, enjoy a happy, safe Fourth of July. And please join me in thanking our law enforcement who are working overtime this holiday weekend to keep our communities safe.

\-- Jim

name: Dean Browning for Congress
subject: Happy Independence Day!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BROWNING FOR CONGRESS
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| Dear Patriot,
All of us from the Campaign Headquarters would like to wish you a Happy Independence Day!
Two-hundred and forty three years ago today our country was founded on the principles of democracy and individual liberty. Principles that were so valued by our Founders they adopted the Bill of Rights to ensure their perseverance through time.
Unfortunately, the extreme liberal establishment seems to forget what this day is all about.
While it has become custom, today is not about going on the lake, grilling hotdogs and hamburgers, and capping off the night with a local fireworks show...
No. Rather, today is a celebration of the freedom that we have and the sacrifices those before have made to protect those freedoms. It is a reminder of our God given rights we have as Americans.
I believe Thomas Jefferson said it best, "Let the annual return of this day forever refresh our recollections of these rights, and an undiminished devotion to them."
I hope each of you has a safe, enjoyable, and patriotic 4th of July.

#
**In 1776,** a group of courageous women and men changed the face of the world forever.

Our Founders stood against tyranny, fought in the trenches, and stared down the enemy. They defied the odds. **They won the fight for America’s independence and built a government like nothing the world had ever seen.**

This government was founded on the principles of economic opportunity, fiscal responsibility, limited government, and individual liberty. **A government of the people, by the people, and for the people – a true representative democracy.**

Freedom came at a high price, and the fight continues today. Thank you to those serving in uniform overseas and their families here at home for defending freedom around the world. **America remains a land of liberty and opportunity because of your service and dedication.**

May God continue to bless the United States of America. Bonnie and I wish each of you a Happy Independence Day.

David

name: Sri Preston Kulkarni
subject: Commemorating July 4, 1776
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sri for Congress
body:
Friends,

While I will not be attending President Trump's military parade today, I am proud to be in our nation's capital for Independence Day. Last night, I celebrated with several friends in the DC area, including my friend, Trevor, a Marine and former Harvard classmate who just got back from Afghanistan.

Commemorating July 4, 1776 brings to mind many concepts of our nation's founding, like being willing to fight for democracy, rejecting a wealthy nobility who rule through family bloodlines, and standing up for basic rights that our rulers cannot infringe on, such as all religions being treated equally, the free press's right to criticize the government, or the right of our people to assemble in protest and speak out against their government.

Personally, the 4th of July reminds me of the tremendous challenge our founders had in trying to form a new type of society. They feared that this experiment in "government by the people" could collapse if there was an executive branch too strong relative to Congress, if a President acted more like a monarch who may not leave office, if elected officials were influenced by personal corruption (especially from foreign powers), or if a demagogue was elected who did not respect democracy. (This last is one of the reasons we have an electoral college, as a buffer in case somehow a person who does not respect our democracy gets past the first round of elections.)

Democracy is not tangible, like a concrete wall, and it is not a number, like the Dow Jones Industrial Average. No one ever risked their life for these things. Those of us willing to risk everything to serve our country do so because we believe in a concept that cannot be seen or quantified. We believe that there is a set of values that holds us together and unites us as society.

Those who fought for our independence did not agree on everything. There were serious conflicts, even at the birth of our nation. But we agreed that we had each other's best interest at heart, that we were one society, and that we were stronger together than we were separate. When our democracy is threatened by forces of division, corruption, or tyranny, we must all be willing to stand up for it, or risk losing what makes America truly great. During the Constitutional Convention, Benjamin Franklin was asked if we would be a republic or a monarchy. His answer: "A Republic, if you can keep it."

Happy Independence Day from Washington DC ya’ll!

Sri Preston Kulkarni
Candidate for Texas' 22nd Congressional District

name: Michael Bennet
subject: Building America
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for America
body:
| |
---
| Team,

Today, in cities, towns, and communities across America, we are celebrating the founding of our great democracy 243 years ago.

In 1776, our Founders stood at the crossroads of the past, the present, and the future. They recognized that for America to survive and thrive, the momentous challenges of their day needed to be faced and overcome.

It was up to them to carve out a new nation, a republic, the likes of which had never been seen before.

[The courage and keen foresight of the Founders has been carried forward in each generation by others who assumed the mantle of forming a more perfect union.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad9d/134673f2/ca43f3/6996b338/1431461762/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/clean-up-corruption?refcode=EM_FR_B1_070419_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=1&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")

Frederick Douglass. Abraham Lincoln. Susan B. Anthony. Elizabeth Cady Stanton. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Margaret Sanger. Harvey Milk. Mae Jemison. Cesar Chavez.

Immigrants like my mother and her parents coming to America after surviving persecution and tyranny.

Ordinary people who stepped forward to accomplish extraordinary things, renewing the strength and hope of our great republic in every generation.

[Team, all of us are Founders, too. All of us are called upon to keep alive the original dream of the Founders who met in Philadelphia 243 years ago -- and make it stronger.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad9d/134673f2/ca43f3/6996b338/1431461762/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/clean-up-corruption?refcode=EM_FR_B1_070419_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=1&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")

Today, as we celebrate the founding of America, let us find the energy and the courage to keep building and rejuvenating the nation we love as we confront the momentous challenges of our time.

With gratitude for all our Founders, past and present.

Michael

name: Tim Ryan
subject: Today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for America
body:
|
---
Today, on the 4th of July, we come together -- with our friends, families, and neighbors -- to celebrate our nation's founding some 243 years ago.

We've been faced with much adversity and challenge throughout our history but we've never allowed it to define us. Brave men and women fought selflessly to guarantee us the freedoms, rights, and privileges that we enjoy.

It is because of their sacrifice and irrevocable love of country that we are able to live freely, with life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness guiding us forward.

I am forever grateful for these men and women -- and work daily -- to emulate their patriotism, dedication, and fierce commitment to protecting our constitutional rights.

Today, and everyday, I am proud to be an American. I am proud to be a Congressman. And I will certainly be proud to be your President.

Please enjoy and have a safe 4th of July!

\- Tim

clean_body: | Today, on the 4th of July, we come together -- with our friends, families, and neighbors -- to celebrate our nation's founding some 243 years ago. We've been faced with much adversity and challenge throughout our history but we've never allowed it to define us. Brave men and women fought selflessly to guarantee us the freedoms, rights, and privileges that we enjoy. It is because of their sacrifice and irrevocable love of country that we are able to live freely, with life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness guiding us forward. I am forever grateful for these men and women -- and work daily -- to emulate their patriotism, dedication, and fierce commitment to protecting our constitutional rights. Today, and everyday, I am proud to be an American. I am proud to be a Congressman. And I will certainly be proud to be your President. body:
We're hoping that wherever you are, the weather is good, the sun is shining, and you've got everything you need for a good BBQ, fireworks show, or anything else you're doing to celebrate. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ed96/45c30650/1707b804b/669d8a12/2371246060/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")

Hey there,

Happy Fourth of July!

We're hoping that wherever you are, the weather is good, the sun is shining, and you've got everything you need for a good BBQ, fireworks show, or anything else you're doing to celebrate (like a quiet night at home, just re-reading the Declaration of Independence).

Maybe, at one of those BBQs, you'll run into some people you don't see too often. Like your cousin, uncle, or a friend of a friend.

Well, every conversation is a great opportunity to talk about Elizabeth and the grassroots movement we're building together! You could mention how...
* This campaign is fully funded by grassroots donors. No wealthy donor fundraisers, no PACs, no federal lobbyists.
* Elizabeth takes a photo with everyone who wants one after her town halls -- and she gets to hear people's stories, face to face. Fun fact: She's now taken over 35,000 of those photos since this campaign started!
* Her Ultra-Millionaire Tax -- on households worth $50 million or more -- will fully pay for Universal Child Care, her student loan debt cancelation plan, universal free public college, and more.
* Speaking of student loan debt, [**you can calculate how much of your debt would be canceled under Elizabeth 's plan right on her website.**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ed96/45c30650/1707b804b/669d8a15/2371246060/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDcwNGVtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/debt?source=20190704em1-full&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
* **[Same with child care-- you can find out how much your family would save!](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ed96/45c30650/1707b804b/669d8a14/2371246060/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDcwNGVtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/kids?source=20190704em1-full&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**
* She's got a dog named Bailey and a husband named Bruce.

[](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ed96/45c30650/1707b804b/669d8a17/2371246060/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LTIwMTlxM2ZyIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDcwNGVtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-2019q3fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190704em1-full")
* She knows how the American economy is stacked against working families, and that's why she went to Washington to lead the fight to create the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, which has already put $12 billion directly in the pockets of people who were cheated by financial institutions.
* She's committed to fighting for big structural change, and she'll fight as hard for you as for her own family.

Happy Fourth!!

Team Warren

A speech on the steps of the Lincoln Memorial, with the front rows reserved for VIPs -- meaning the Republican elite and the president's political donors.- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Rows of troops. Tanks. Helicopters and fighter jets. A speech on the steps of the Lincoln Memorial, with the front rows reserved for VIPs -- meaning the Republican elite and the president's political donors. **Nothing about Trump 's 4th of July "Salute to America" says "We the People."** President Trump has hijacked a national holiday and turned it into an indulgent, vain tribute to himself that eerily resembles the militaristic displays of repressive regimes around the world. [](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d072b/72412298/43fdfd49/61025402/253543973/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/save_july_4th?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190704_July4th_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190704_July4th_U1_X1") Instruments of war are not toys. The members of the military are not political props. Instead of honoring the values at the foundation of this country -- freedom, tolerance, and respect for all -- Trump is only looking to boost his own ego. [**Denounce Trump 's 4th of July Takeover: Add your name to tell Trump our nation's strength comes from its people, not its military might >>**](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d072b/72412298/43fdfd49/61025405/253543973/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/save_july_4th?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=2+-+Denounce+Trumprsquos+4th+of+July+Takeove&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190704_July4th_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190704_July4th_U1_X1") | | | [Sign on ](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d072b/72412298/43fdfd49/61025404/253543973/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/save_july_4th?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=3+-+Sign+on+&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190704_July4th_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190704_July4th_U1_X1")

Independence Day should be a time for all Americans to come together and celebrate freedom. **We need to speak out against this wasteful display that betrays the values of democracy.**

name: Team Hickenlooper
subject: Today.
body:
Here on Team Hickenlooper, we're lucky that, each and every day, we get to see the very best of what this country has to offer--in the form of your kindness, your enthusiasm, and your determination to | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377177e/691c9bd/6d744731/11a40b73/1683947822/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend, as you celebrate Independence Day today and this holiday weekend with your friends and family, we wanted to send a quick note.

**As we experience a growing crisis of division under Donald Trump, it can sometimes be hard to recognize the country we live in.**

But here on Team Hickenlooper, we're lucky that, each and every day, we get to see the very best of what this country has to offer--in the form of _your_ kindness, _your_ enthusiasm, and _your_ determination to set things right.

**We are so proud and grateful to have you on this team. This Fourth of July, you are what makes this country the best nation in the world.**

We hope you enjoy this important day with your loved ones--with parades, picnics, fireworks, or maybe just some watermelon, beer, and music. Whatever you're doing, let's all make sure to take a moment to reflect on those who got us here, and the importance of moving forward--together--in their honor.

Thank you for everything you do. Let's keep up the work.

Giddy up,
Team Hickenlooper
---

---

| | | Friend,

I wanted to take a moment to wish you and yours a wonderful Fourth of July holiday.

[](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113dda/622b7b5b/103515df/307b0559/3015676400/VEsH/ "https://debbiewassermanschultz.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_EN_90DO_190704_1_1&source=DWS_EM_EN_90DO_190704_1_1")

Best,

Debbie

name: Cory Booker
subject: She was so hungry while in detention that she said they would eat the peel of an orange.
body:
| | |
---
| Friend, Today's our nation's birthday, a day to celebrate the ideals and values we strive for, together. Right now, I can't get my mind off of something profoundly inhumane happening at our border. Yesterday, I witnessed the impact our broken immigration system has on those we should be protecting. At the request of immigrant rights groups Families Belong Together and Las Americas Immigrant Advocacy Center, I crossed into Ciudad de Juarez, Mexico, to help five women present themselves for asylum. I pray that they are allowed to stay in the United States. Some of these women had been turned away on previous attempts to cross, and the groups informed me that a senator escorting asylum seekers is the best chance they have to be allowed to make their case to immigration officials. That's our absurd reality -- we must instead be a nation that welcomes those seeking refuge from violence. During our time together, I got to know these women through the stories they shared. One of them described the month she spent in detention. While there, she said the entire detention center had to stand in the sun all day without food, just water -- but sometimes they ran out of water too. Another -- I can't share her name out of concern for her safety -- had to leave her home under threat of rape. She was so hungry while in detention that she said they ate the peel of an orange. A third woman has bruises all over her back from sleeping on the hard floor of the detention center. She wasn't able to shower for over 20 days and has rashes on her skin from the lack of sanitation. While in the detention center, she became sick. Despite the fact that the doctor wanted her to go to the hospital, the border patrol refused. She was put in isolation and thought she would die. **These stories are profoundly alarming, but my words can 't begin to capture the pain. Their very human dignity is under assault, and it's being done in our name.** This crisis was created by the Trump Administration's deliberately cruel, inhumane policies. These policies target already vulnerable people and put them at risk of even more danger. It goes without saying that it's wrong, immoral, and in direct conflict with our ideals. When I'm president, I won't wait to fix our immigration policies -- on Day One, I'll take executive action to end the Trump Administration's border policies, including closing the camps holding thousands of human beings in inhumane conditions. **But those actions in January 2021 won't do much to help the thousands of people who need relief right now. People, including children, are dying in these inhumane conditions -- and we can 't wait to act. In thanks to Families Belong Together and Las Americas Immigrant Advocacy Center for the chance to witness the crisis at our border firsthand, I'd like to give back. [So join me -- support some of the amazing organizations doing the work to help immigrants right now. Make a donation to be split among Families Belong Together, Immigration Equality, Las Americas Immigrant Advocacy Center, UndocuBlack Network, and Women's Refugee Commission. 100% of your donation will go directly toward these groups and the vital work they do.](https://act.corybooker. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190704-em?refcode=em_fr_20190704_CB_20190704-fr-CB-immigration-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") ** I know today President Trump is planning a big celebration wasting and misusing taxpayer dollars and military resources on what has become a political rally in Washington, D.C. True patriotism has nothing to do with what he's planning today. It's not a taxpayer-funded Trump rally that will define America this July 4th. It must be our actions. And the most patriotic act I can think of right now is to speak out, forcefully, for the people suffering at our border. For the families who've been ripped apart or who fear they will be by this administration. For the people fleeing persecution or violence in search of a better life for themselves and their families. What's more American than that? Hear me clearly: We will fight with everything we've got to end this moral crisis. And we will prevail. God bless America,
Cory _A quick note on this email: this is not an endorsement of Cory Booker for president by any of the groups your donation will be split with. 100% of your contribution will go directly to Families Belong Together, Immigration Equality, Las Americas Immigrant Advocacy Center, UndocuBlack Network, and Women 's Refugee Commission._

---

This campaign is moving quickly. It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us.
